heading: Statement of purpose; authorization of appropriations
label: 1381 Statement of purpose; authorization of appropriations
text: For the purpose of establishing a national program to provide supplemental security income to individuals who have attained age 65 or are blind or disabled, there are authorized to be appropriated sums sufficient to carry out this subchapter.
notes: Prior ProvisionsA prior section 1381, , as added , , , authorized appropriations for grants to States for aid to aged, blind, or disabled, and for medical assistance for aged, prior to the general amendment of title XVI of the Social Security Act by , but is set out as a note below in view of its continued applicability to Puerto Rico, Guam, and the Virgin Islands.act Aug. 14, 1935, ch. 531, title XVI, § 1601Pub. Application to Northern Mariana IslandsFor applicability of this section to the Northern Mariana Islands, see section 502(a)(1) of the Covenant to Establish a Commonwealth of the Northern Mariana Islands in Political Union with the United States of America and Proc. No. 4534, , 42 F.R. 6593, set out as notes under , Territories and Insular Possessions.Oct. 24, 1977section 1801 of Title 48

Puerto Rico, Guam, and Virgin IslandsEnactment of section 1601 of the Social Security Act by , eff. (), was not applicable to Puerto Rico, Guam, and the Virgin Islands. See , set out as a note under . Therefore, as to Puerto Rico, Guam, and the Virgin Islands, section 1601 of the Social Security Act as it existed prior to reenactment by (former ), and as amended, continues to apply and reads as follows:Pub. L. 92–60342 U.S.C. 1381section 303(b) of Pub. L. 92–603section 301 of this titlePub. L. 92–60342 U.S.C. 1381Jan. 1, 1974§ 1381. Authorization of appropriationsFor the purpose of enabling each State, as far as practicable under the conditions in such State, to furnish financial assistance to needy individuals who are 65 years of age or over, are blind, or are 18 years of age or over and permanently and totally disabled, there is hereby authorized to be appropriated for each fiscal year a sum sufficient to carry out the purposes of this subchapter. The sums made available under this section shall be used for making payments to States which have submitted, and had approved by the Commissioner of Social Security, State plans for aid to the aged, blind, or disabled.(, title XVI, § 1601, as added , , ; amended , , ; , , title XXIII, § 2353(m)(1), , 873; , , .)Aug. 14, 1935, ch. 531Pub. Payments Under Chapter Provisions in Effect Before , for: Activities Carried Out Through , Under State Plans Approved Under Subchapter I, X, XIV, or XVI Provisions; and for Administrative Activities After , Closing Out Such ActivitiesJanuary 1, 1974December 31, 1973January 1, 1974, , , provided that:Pub. L. 93–233, § 19(b)87 Stat. 974Dec. 31, 1973“Notwithstanding the provisions of section 301 of the Social Security Amendments of 1972 [enacting this subchapter], the Secretary of Health, Education, and Welfare [now Health and Human Services] shall make payments to the 50 States and the District of Columbia after , in accordance with the provisions of the Social Security Act [ et seq.] as in effect prior to , for (1) activities carried out through the close of , under State plans approved under title I, X, XIV, or XVI, of such Act [ et seq., 1201 et seq., 1351 et seq., 1381 et seq.], and (2) administrative activities carried out after , which such Secretary determines are necessary to bring to a close activities carried out under such State plans.”December 31, 1973January 1, 1974December 31, 1973December 31, 197342 U.S.C. 30142 U.S.C. 301
